text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check small, individual pieces of code to ensure each part works correctly on its own. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work well together. Many developers use test-driven development, or TDD, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps catch problems early and makes the code easier to maintain. Ultimately, thorough testing gives developers confidence that their software behaves as expected. With a solid test suite, they can make changes and add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality. This trust is essential for efficient and reliable development.
